100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On March 22, 1926, a significant earthquake struck the region of Central Italy, causing widespread destruction and prompting urgent rescue efforts. In political news, British Prime Minister Stanley Baldwin addressed Parliament, emphasizing the need for economic stability amid rising tensions in Europe. Meanwhile, in the United States, the Senate debated the merits of the proposed McNary-Haugen Bill, aimed at supporting struggling farmers through government intervention.
